intent: Keep a sustainable daily rhythm while shipping on the current cluster of projects (autodoro, RSI variance, intender-app, sandboxing).
finding: Marathon-day pattern has not eased. Across the last 11 days (04-09 through 04-19), active-user-message spans sit at 11-16 hours on 9 of those days, with session counts of 14-44 per heavy day. April 19 alone: 07:33 -> 22:47 local, 172 user messages across 15 distinct sessions. This is the exact pattern the 2026-04-12 monthly review already flagged and proposed to address with session-length awareness; proposal is marked implemented but the behavioural pattern has continued at the same intensity. Tension: high frequency and long duration, but no explicit user frustration in the logs — keeping tier at high rather than critical.
existing_mitigation: 2026-04-12-marathon-sessions.md (status: implemented); ~/.claude/wellbeing/work-hours-nudge.py; autodoro pomodoro service in ~/Repos/autodoro. Mitigations exist but do not appear to be altering the underlying rhythm.

intent: Finish in-flight work at the end of the day without spilling past 23:00 or crossing midnight.
finding: Late-night work (>=23:00 or <06:00 local Europe/Stockholm) is a repeated signature, not a one-off. Counts of user messages in the late-night window over the last two weeks: 04-07 -> 44, 04-09 -> 10, 04-10 -> 6, 04-11 -> 56, 04-14 -> 16, 04-15 -> 33 (all within 00:00-00:33, i.e. a midnight-crossing tail from the previous day), 04-17 -> 5. That is 7 of the last 13 days with meaningful late-night activity, and two nights (04-11 and 04-07) where the late-night block is itself a substantial work session. The wellbeing category explicitly lists 'after 11pm' as an observable manic-mode signature. The 04-12 marathon proposal addressed overall span but did not specifically target the late-evening tail.
existing_mitigation: work-hours-nudge.py and sunset-walk-nudge.py exist in ~/.claude/wellbeing/, but have not prevented the late-night tails. No hook currently warns at 23:00 or at midnight crossing.

intent: Make progress across several concurrent projects without fragmenting attention.
finding: High session fragmentation on heavy days: 23 sessions on 04-18, 39 on 04-16, 44 on 04-17, 35 on 04-12, 37 on 04-11. On 04-19 two long-span sessions ran in overlapping time windows: b6aa1d47 (09:35 -> 22:47, 63 user messages) and afc7c920 (11:56 -> 19:24, 25 messages), in addition to 13 other shorter sessions. That matches the categories.md manic-mode signature 'short intense bursts followed by abrupt stops' combined with context-switching. Not every session-switch is waste — some are intentional separation by project — but the density is high enough to be worth naming.
existing_mitigation: None specific to session fragmentation. Marathon-session proposal measured aggregate time, not switch count.

intent: Maintain consecutive-day stamina without accumulating fatigue across a streak of long workdays.
finding: Consecutive-long-day streak: 04-16 (14.3h), 04-17 (16.3h, plus late-night tail), 04-18 (14.0h), 04-19 (15.2h). Four long days in a row, each with 100+ user messages. The 2026-04-12 monthly review flagged 'two 10+ hour days in a row' as the threshold worth noticing; the current streak has doubled that. Calibration: previous marathon finding was written at 'implemented, no-action-needed' tone, so the same observation written again risks being re-rejected — but the doubling of the streak is substantively new evidence, so it is worth recording at medium rather than skipping.
existing_mitigation: 2026-04-12-marathon-sessions.md covers the concept but did not include a consecutive-day threshold. autodoro addresses within-session breaks, not across-day streaks.
